div.p: To all Christ’s faithful people who shall view or hear this writing, Patrick son of William wishes everlasting salvation in the Lord. May you all know the lord abbot and convent of Scone to have made satisfaction to me in respect of all manner of debts in which they were obliged to me, by written deeds as well as by any other contract, in respect of the livestock and fermes of Blair, and all other agreements, loans, or any other contracts; and to have renounced for myself and my heirs all loans which I have or could have in relation to them, in respect of any contract of the aforesaid. Wherefore I will that if any instrument be found concerning any contract entered into between us, and especially in respect of Blair, it be of no value or force. In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to this writing together with the seal of the venerable father, Geoffrey, bishop of Dunkeld. Given at Cargill on the Day of Saint Augustine, bishop of the English, in the Year of Grace 1246. As witnesses: the lord Malcolm, precentor of Dunkeld; Master John of Everley, canon of Dunkeld; the lord Peter of Cargill; William of Clunie; Simon the clerk, and many others.
